senses.definition: causing someone to be absorbed or engrossed in thoughts or concerns
examples: The news of the rescue was preoccupying her mind all day.
He was preoccupying himself with doubts about the project's viability.

senses.definition: to happen before another event or before something else is expected to occur
examples: A minor incident preoccurs before the main crisis.
Several symptoms preoccur during the early stages of the disease.

senses.definition: happened before another event or earlier than expected
examples: The warning preoccurred before the main disaster.
A preoccurred event set the tone for the investigation.

senses.definition: an event or situation that happens before something else, often causing concern or forewarning
examples: The preoccurrence of storms led to evacuations.
Historians study the preoccurrence of the conflict.

senses.definition: occurring beforehand; happening prior to another event
examples: preoccurring symptoms suggested an underlying condition.
We examined preoccurring factors before the decision.

senses.definition: relating to the area before the ocean; not a standard common term, used rarely in specialized historical or geological contexts
examples: Preoceanic sediments were studied by the geologist.
Some models include a preoceanic era in the planet's formation.

senses.definition: situated in front of the eye or relating to the area in front of the eye; used primarily in anatomy
examples: Preocular muscles control eye movement.
The term preocular is used in certain anatomical diagrams.

senses.definition: before emitting a noticeable odor; smelling earlier in a sequence
examples: The plant has a preodorous scent that signals when to harvest.
Preodorous hints suggested a change in the atmosphere.

senses.definition: situated before the esophagus in an anatomical sequence; relating to the area anterior to the esophagus
examples: Preoesophageal structures were identified during dissection.
The preoesophageal region is studied in comparative anatomy.

senses.definition: to cause someone to feel offended beforehand; to anticipate offense and act to prevent it
examples: The speaker tried to preoffend potential critics by acknowledging concerns.
They issued a clarification to preoffend sensitive audiences.

senses.definition: an act or state before an offense; a prior wrongdoing or anticipated wrongdoing
examples: The policy reduces preoffense by addressing concerns early.
They discussed preoffense in the context of crime prevention.

senses.definition: intended to prevent offense; aimed at avoiding insulting or provocative content
examples: The preoffensive wording avoided sensitive terms.
A preoffensive approach helps maintain respectful dialogue.

senses.definition: in a manner intended to prevent offense or avoid insult
examples: The speaker spoke preoffensively to avoid upsetting the audience.
They preoffensively reformulated the policy.

senses.definition: the quality of being designed to prevent offense; caution against insults
examples: The preoffensiveness of the guidelines reduced hostility.
Publications often aim for preoffensiveness in sensitive topics.

senses.definition: to offer something before another party; to propose or present in advance
examples: The company preoffers discounts to loyal customers.
They preoffer a trial period to attract buyers.

senses.definition: an offering made in advance; an initial proposal or preview
examples: The preoffering attracted early investors.
We reviewed the preoffering before the main launch.

senses.definition: before an official role or designation; not yet officially recognized
examples: The preofficial status was noted in the draft agreement.
They arranged a preofficial briefing for stakeholders.

senses.definition: in a manner before official recognition or designation
examples: The team operated preofficially until the contract was signed.
The project was preofficially announced to stakeholders.

senses.definition: to dominate or be more prominent before others; to preemerge in importance
examples: Cultural biases tend to preominate in early discussions.
A few ideas preominate during the planning phase.

senses.definition: the act of omitting something beforehand; an intentional exclusion
examples: The preomission of a detail changed the interpretation.
Preomission in reporting can mislead readers.

senses.definition: to omit beforehand; to leave out in advance
examples: We preomit irrelevant sections to streamline the report.
The editor preomitted the awkward anecdote.

senses.definition: omitted beforehand; left out in advance
examples: A preomitted clause could affect liability.
The preomitted names were later added in an appendix.

senses.definition: the act of omitting beforehand; the act of leaving out in advance
examples: Preomitting unnecessary details speeds up the narrative.
They are preomitting sensitive information.

senses.definition: a hypothetical subatomic particle; a term used in speculative physics
examples: The model proposed preons as constituents of quarks.
Preons are a theoretical concept awaiting experimental proof.

senses.definition: plural of preon; hypothetical subatomic particles
examples: Various preons were proposed to explain quark structure.
Experiments test the possible existence of preons.
